task_completion.description: Open the target image in an image editor
task_completion.visual_evidence: A portrait image (or fallback image) is loaded and visible in the main workspace of a graphical image editor like GIMP.

task_completion.description: Open the Unsharp Mask filter tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Unsharp Mask' dialog box is open on the screen (in GIMP, typically accessed via Filters > Enhance > Sharpen (Unsharp Mask)).

task_completion.description: Adjust the Unsharp Mask parameters to enhance detail
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Unsharp Mask dialog shows active interaction, with values for Radius, Amount, and/or Threshold set to provide noticeable sharpening without extreme distortion.

task_completion.description: Apply the Unsharp Mask filter to the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Unsharp Mask dialog is closed and the image in the workspace appears visibly sharper with enhanced edge definition compared to the original.

task_completion.description: Save or export the sharpened image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save', 'Export', or 'Overwrite' dialog is visible, or a status message indicates the modified image has been saved to disk.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical image editor rather than terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a GUI application (like GIMP), not just a terminal window running ImageMagick commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened and used the Unsharp Mask dialog
integrity.visual_evidence: The specific 'Unsharp Mask' dialog window is clearly visible in at least one screenshot, proving the correct filter was selected.

integrity.description: The agent avoided extreme settings that would cause programmatic failure due to over-sharpening
integrity.visual_evidence: The final image looks naturally enhanced; it does not have severe noise amplification, extreme bright/dark halos around edges, or blown-out contrast.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is typically an Unsplash portrait photo (800x1200). The task requires applying an 'Unsharp Mask' filter, typically in GIMP. A successful application should visibly increase sharpness and edge definition without causing severe over-sharpening (which the programmatic verifier strictly checks by failing contrast increases >80% or noise amplification >50%). GIMP's default Unsharp Mask settings are Radius 5.0, Amount 0.5, Threshold 0.
